summary: Elder Walker teaches that those who obey the Savior’s voice and faithfully keep their covenants become the Lord’s “peculiar treasure,” receiving His acceptance, strength, and covenant blessings.
haiku:
Covenants bind hearts
Treasured by the Living Christ
We rise through His love
key_points: The Lord invites His people to become His peculiar treasure by obeying His voice and keeping His covenants.
Covenant faithfulness brings divine power, closeness to God, and strength to rise above the world.
The Lord accepts honest, broken, and contrite hearts that are willing to sacrifice in order to honor covenants.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Exodus 19:5 — becoming a peculiar treasure by obeying God’s voice and keeping His covenant
Moses 1:39 — God’s work and glory is our immortality and eternal life
John 15:13 — the Savior’s supreme love shown through His sacrifice
Doctrine and Covenants 97:8 — honest hearts, broken spirits, and covenant sacrifice are accepted of the Lord
President Russell M. Nelson’s teachings on the covenant relationship with God
President Dallin H. Oaks’s teachings on honoring covenants and rising to celestial heights
President D. Todd Christofferson’s teachings on covenant blessings and divine power
questions_to_ponder: What does it mean to me personally to be the Lord’s peculiar treasure?
How can I better obey the Savior’s voice in daily decisions?
What sacrifices might the Lord be inviting me to make so I can keep my covenants more fully?
When have I felt strengthened or sustained because of my covenants?
How can remembering God’s covenant promises help me face trials with greater hope?
